# Runics

> Semantic skill registry for AI agents. Indexes 15,000+ agent skills from the MCP Registry, ClawHub, GitHub, and human-curated sources. Every skill is security-scanned by Cognium and ranked by a trust score. Free semantic search API, no auth required.

Runics is operated by Cognium Labs. It exists to solve a specific problem: agent skill directories (MCP Registry, ClawHub, etc.) list skills but do not tell an agent which ones are safe or which version to pick. Runics adds (1) semantic search instead of keyword matching, (2) security trust scores from continuous vulnerability and prompt-injection scanning, (3) multi-source aggregation, and (4) confidence-tiered routing so fast queries stay fast and ambiguous queries get deeper analysis.

## What Runics is

- **Type**: Cloud API and web interface
- **Audience**: AI agents, agent developers, LLM tool integrators
- **Pricing**: Free for search, no authentication required
- **API base URL**: https://api.runics.net
- **Web interface**: https://runics.net

## How search works

Search is routed through three tiers by confidence:

- **Tier 1 — Vector similarity**: HNSW over pgvector embeddings (bge-small-en-v1.5). Sub-60ms p50. Returned immediately when top results are clearly relevant.
- **Tier 2 — Cross-encoder reranking**: When confidence is medium, top candidates are reranked by a cross-encoder model for better precision.
- **Tier 3 — LLM deep search**: When a query is complex or ambiguous, an LLM performs query decomposition and composition detection, and may invoke semantic search with alternate phrasings.

Every response includes: `confidence` (high | medium | low_enriched | no_match), `meta.tier` (1 | 2 | 3), `meta.latencyMs`, `meta.reranked`, `meta.llmInvoked`, and `meta.matchSources` (which embedding types matched).

## Trust scoring

Every indexed skill is scanned by **Cognium** for:

- Known vulnerabilities (CVE matching)
- License issues
- Unsafe permissions
- Prompt-injection patterns
- Supply chain indicators

Trust scores are 0–1 floats and update continuously. Versions are ranked by `trust × 0.7 + usage × 0.3`, so the most reliable version surfaces first rather than the newest.

## Sources indexed

- MCP Registry (Anthropic Model Context Protocol)
- ClawHub
- GitHub repositories tagged as agent skills
- Human-curated skills
- Additional adapters via `src/sync/`

## API

### Search

```
POST https://api.runics.net/v1/search
Content-Type: application/json

{"query": "web scraping", "tenantId": "public"}
```

Returns a `FindSkillResponse` with `results`, `confidence`, `meta`, and optional `composition`, `searchTrace`, `generationHints`.

### Leaderboards

- `GET /v1/leaderboards/human` — ranked by human stars
- `GET /v1/leaderboards/agents` — ranked by agent invocation score
- `GET /v1/leaderboards/trending` — ranked by weekly invocations
- `GET /v1/leaderboards/most-composed` — ranked by composition inclusion count
- `GET /v1/leaderboards/most-forked` — ranked by fork count

Query params: `type`, `category`, `ecosystem`, `limit` (1–100, default 20), `offset`.

### Health

`GET https://api.runics.net/health`

## Stack

- TypeScript on Cloudflare Workers (Hono framework)
- Neon Postgres with pgvector (HNSW) and tsvector
- Cloudflare Workers AI for embeddings, reranking, and LLM fallback
- Cloudflare KV for caching
- Hyperdrive for Postgres connection pooling

## Key differentiators

- Only agent skill registry with semantic search + security trust scoring
- Only registry that aggregates MCP Registry, ClawHub, and GitHub in a single query
- Only registry returning confidence signals and per-result trust/verification metadata
- Only registry with automatic composition detection for multi-skill queries
